45	// serverPromptCapBytes caps the size of inline prompt bodies accepted on
46	// server-side ingress (POST /api/agent/run). FEAT-022 §9 / Stage D2. It is
47	// a package-level variable rather than a literal so tests can lower the
48	// cap without writing multi-MB request bodies. Production callers must not
49	// mutate it.
50	var serverPromptCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxPromptBytes
51	
52	// serverInlineCapBytes caps inline content bodies returned on egress paths
53	// — MCP tool responses and REST JSON responses that carry library-doc,
54	// execution-bundle, diff, session, or persona text. FEAT-022 §10 / §11.
55	// Stage E1. Package-level variable so tests can lower the cap; production
56	// callers must not mutate it.
57	var serverInlineCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxInlinedFileBytes
58	
59	// mcpText constructs a text-typed mcpContent whose Text body is bounded by
60	// serverInlineCapBytes via evidence.ClampOutput. The Truncated and
61	// OriginalBytes fields are always populated (false/len(text) when the body
62	// fits within the cap). This is the single mcpContent{Type:"text"} literal
63	// site in this package — every other caller must route through this helper
64	// so static analysis (TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage) can guarantee no
65	// unbounded text egress on the MCP surface. FEAT-022 §10 / Stage E1.
66	func mcpText(text string) mcpContent {
67		clamped, truncated, originalBytes := evidence.ClampOutput(text, serverInlineCapBytes)
68		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="single mcpContent{Type:\"text\"} literal site for the package; Text is sourced from evidence.ClampOutput above (FEAT-022 §10 Stage E1)"
69		return mcpContent{Type: "text", Text: clamped, Truncated: truncated, OriginalBytes: originalBytes}
70	}

76		Close()

79	// Server is the DDx HTTP server exposing REST and MCP endpoints.
80	type Server struct {
81		Addr        string
82		WorkingDir  string
83		TsnetConfig *config.TsnetConfig
84		mux         *http.ServeMux
85		startTime   time.Time
86		workers     *WorkerManager

90		// csrfTokens issues and validates CSRF tokens for write mutations on
91		// the GraphQL endpoint (Story 15 / operator-prompts). The token is
92		// generated once at server construction and exposed to clients via
93		// GET /api/csrf-token (localhost-gated).
94		csrfTokens *ddxgraphql.StaticCSRFTokenStore
95		// operatorPromptIdempotency deduplicates operatorPromptSubmit calls by
96		// idempotency key within a 24-hour window. Process-local; survives
97		// only for the lifetime of the server process.
98		operatorPromptIdempotency *ddxgraphql.MemoryIdempotencyCache
99		// operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist is the per-project allowlist of
100		// localhost identity actors permitted to auto-approve their own
101		// operator-prompt submissions and to invoke operatorPromptApprove. The
102		// locked Story 15 decision restricts approval to configured-localhost
103		// identities; ts-net peers are never eligible. Seeded from
104		// DDX_OPERATOR_PROMPT_ALLOWLIST (comma-separated) at server construction
105		// and may be overridden by tests via SetOperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist.
106		operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist []string
107	
108		routePatterns []string // every pattern registered via route(); used by gate tests
109	
110		// HubMode is true once EnableHubMode has been called; the federation
111		// HTTP routes are mounted only in that case (B14.3 AC: "Routes mounted
112		// only when --hub-mode").
113		HubMode bool
114		hub     *federationHub
115

117		// with HubMode to compute node.federation_role:
118		//   neither   → "standalone"
119		//   hub only  → "hub"
120		//   spoke only→ "spoke"
121		//   both      → "hub_spoke"
122		SpokeMode bool

126	// New creates a new DDx server bound to addr, serving data from workingDir.
127	func New(addr, workingDir string) *Server {
128		nodeName := resolveNodeName()
129		stateDir := serverAddrDir() // XDG-standard user-level dir, one per node
130		state := loadServerState(stateDir, nodeName)
131		state.workingDir = workingDir
132	
133		workers := NewWorkerManager(workingDir)
134		workers.ReconcileStaleWorkers()
135		beadHub := bead.NewWatcherHub(250 * time.Millisecond)
136		csrfStore, err := ddxgraphql.NewStaticCSRFTokenStore()
137		if err != nil {
138			// CSRF token generation should never fail outside of a broken
139			// crypto/rand. Fall back to a deterministic stub that rejects
140			// every request rather than failing open.
141			csrfStore = ddxgraphql.NewStaticCSRFTokenStoreWithToken("")
142		}
143		s := &Server{
144			Addr:                               addr,
145			WorkingDir:                         workingDir,
146			mux:                                http.NewServeMux(),
147			startTime:                          time.Now().UTC(),
148			workers:                            workers,
149			beadHub:                            beadHub,
150			state:                              state,
151			csrfTokens:                         csrfStore,
152			operatorPromptIdempotency:          ddxgraphql.NewMemoryIdempotencyCache(),
153			operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist: parseOperatorPromptAllowlistEnv(os.Getenv("DDX_OPERATOR_PROMPT_ALLOWLIST")),
154		}
155		state.coordinatorReg = workers.LandCoordinators
156	
157		// Register the server's own project immediately.
158		state.RegisterProject(workingDir)
159		_ = state.save()
160	
161		s.routes()
162		return s
163	}
164	
165	// parseOperatorPromptAllowlistEnv parses a comma-separated identity list
166	// (DDX_OPERATOR_PROMPT_ALLOWLIST) into a normalized allowlist. Empty entries
167	// are skipped; whitespace is trimmed. The literal sentinel "localhost"
168	// matches any localhost-kind actor.
169	func parseOperatorPromptAllowlistEnv(raw string) []string {
170		if raw = strings.TrimSpace(raw); raw == "" {
171			return nil
172		}
173		parts := strings.Split(raw, ",")
174		out := make([]string, 0, len(parts))
175		for _, p := range parts {
176			p = strings.TrimSpace(p)
177			if p == "" {
178				continue
179			}
180			out = append(out, p)
181		}
182		return out
183	}
184	
185	// SetOperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist replaces the in-memory allowlist
186	// used by operatorPromptSubmit (with autoApprove=true) and operatorPromptApprove.
187	// Tests use this to install a deterministic allowlist without juggling env vars.
188	func (s *Server) SetOperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist(allowlist []string) {
189		s.operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist = append([]string(nil), allowlist...)
190	}

192	// resolveNodeName returns DDX_NODE_NAME env, or the system hostname.
193	func resolveNodeName() string {
194		if n := os.Getenv("DDX_NODE_NAME"); n != "" {
195			return n
196		}
197		if h, err := os.Hostname(); err == nil && h != "" {
198			return h
199		}
200		return "unknown"
201	}

204	func (s *Server) Handler() http.Handler {
205		return s.mux
206	}

208	// State exposes the server's persistent state so adjacent packages (notably
209	// the perf harness) can register additional projects or query snapshots
210	// without reaching into unexported internals. The returned value MUST NOT be
211	// mutated concurrently with the server — it is the same pointer the server
212	// itself uses.
213	func (s *Server) State() *ServerState {
214		return s.state
215	}

217	// RegisterProject adds (or refreshes) a project entry on the server's state
218	// and persists the updated state file. Returns the stored entry.
219	func (s *Server) RegisterProject(path string) ProjectEntry {
220		entry := s.state.RegisterProject(path)
221		_ = s.state.save()
222		return entry
223	}

225	// Shutdown stops the server's background services: closes the bead lifecycle
226	// hub and stops all land coordinators. Returns the first error encountered.
227	// Both operations are idempotent and safe to call on an idle server.
228	func (s *Server) Shutdown() error {
229		// Best-effort spoke deregister so the hub registry does not show this
230		// node as stale after a graceful shutdown. Errors are swallowed inside
231		// ShutdownSpoke (the hub may legitimately be down).
232		if s.spoke != nil {
233			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 3*time.Second)
234			_ = s.ShutdownSpoke(ctx)
235			cancel()
236		}
237		s.beadHub.Close()
238		s.workers.LandCoordinators.StopAll()
239		return nil
240	}

242	// ListenAndServe starts the server. If TsnetConfig.Enabled is true, a parallel
243	// Tailscale ts-net listener is started alongside the standard localhost listener.
244	func (s *Server) ListenAndServe() error {
245		release, err := acquireSingletonLock()
246		if err != nil {
247			return err
248		}
249		defer release()
250		s.installSingletonReleaseOnSignal(release)
251		s.writeAddrFile("http")
252		if s.TsnetConfig != nil && s.TsnetConfig.Enabled {
253			errCh := make(chan error, 2)
254	
255			// Standard localhost listener
256			go func() {
257				errCh <- http.ListenAndServe(s.Addr, s.mux)
258			}()
259	
260			// ts-net listener
261			go func() {
262				errCh <- s.listenTsnet()
263			}()
264	
265			return <-errCh
266		}
267		return http.ListenAndServe(s.Addr, s.mux)
268	}
269	
270	// ListenAndServeTLS starts the server with TLS. If certFile and keyFile are
271	// empty, a self-signed certificate is auto-generated and cached under
272	// workingDir/.ddx/server/tls/.
273	func (s *Server) ListenAndServeTLS(certFile, keyFile string) error {
274		release, err := acquireSingletonLock()
275		if err != nil {
276			return err
277		}
278		defer release()
279		s.installSingletonReleaseOnSignal(release)
280		if certFile == "" || keyFile == "" {
281			var err error
282			tlsDir := filepath.Join(s.WorkingDir, ".ddx", "server", "tls")
283			certFile, keyFile, err = ensureSelfSignedCert(tlsDir)
284			if err != nil {
285				return fmt.Errorf("generating self-signed cert: %w", err)
286			}
287		}
288		s.writeAddrFile("https")
289		if s.TsnetConfig != nil && s.TsnetConfig.Enabled {
290			errCh := make(chan error, 2)
291			go func() {
292				errCh <- http.ListenAndServeTLS(s.Addr, certFile, keyFile, s.mux)
293			}()
294			go func() {
295				errCh <- s.listenTsnet()
296			}()
297			return <-errCh
298		}
299		return http.ListenAndServeTLS(s.Addr, certFile, keyFile, s.mux)
300	}
301	
302	// writeAddrFile writes the server's address to a user-level file so CLI
303	// clients can auto-discover it without configuration.
304	func (s *Server) writeAddrFile(scheme string) {
305		type addrFile struct {
306			Node      string    `json:"node"`
307			NodeID    string    `json:"node_id"`
308			URL       string    `json:"url"`
309			PID       int       `json:"pid"`
310			StartedAt time.Time `json:"started_at"`
311		}
312		af := addrFile{
313			Node:      s.state.Node.Name,
314			NodeID:    s.state.Node.ID,
315			URL:       fmt.Sprintf("%s://%s", scheme, s.Addr),
316			PID:       os.Getpid(),
317			StartedAt: s.startTime,
318		}
319		data, err := json.MarshalIndent(af, "", "  ")
320		if err != nil {
321			return
322		}
323		dir := serverAddrDir()
324		if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0700); err != nil {
325			return
326		}
327		_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "server.addr"), data, 0600)
328	}
329	
330	// serverAddrDir returns the user-level directory for the server address file.
331	// Follows XDG_DATA_HOME if set, else ~/.local/share/ddx.
332	func serverAddrDir() string {
333		if xdg := os.Getenv("XDG_DATA_HOME"); xdg != "" {
334			return filepath.Join(xdg, "ddx")
335		}
336		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
337		if err != nil {
338			return filepath.Join("/tmp", "ddx")
339		}
340		return filepath.Join(home, ".local", "share", "ddx")
341	}
342	
343	// ReadServerAddr reads the last-written server address from the user-level
344	// addr file. Returns "" if none is present, or if the recorded pid is not
345	// alive (in which case a warning is emitted and the stale file is best-effort
346	// cleared so callers fall back to the default URL).
347	func ReadServerAddr() string {
348		type addrFile struct {
349			URL string `json:"url"`
350			PID int    `json:"pid"`
351		}
352		path := filepath.Join(serverAddrDir(), "server.addr")
353		data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
354		if err != nil {
355			return ""
356		}
357		var af addrFile
358		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &af); err != nil {
359			return ""
360		}
361		if af.PID > 0 && !processAlive(af.PID) {
362			fmt.Fprintf(addrFallbackWarnWriter,
363				"warning: server.addr points to a dead pid %d; falling back to default 127.0.0.1:7743\n",
364				af.PID)
365			_ = os.Remove(path)
366			return ""
367		}
368		return af.URL
369	}
370	
371	// installSingletonReleaseOnSignal arranges for the singleton lock to be
372	// released on SIGTERM/SIGINT so that the next ddx-server can start without
373	// having to wait for stale-lock detection. The handler exits the process
374	// with a conventional non-zero status; defers in ListenAndServe(TLS) do not
375	// run, but the lock release fires explicitly here.
376	func (s *Server) installSingletonReleaseOnSignal(release func()) {
377		ch := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
378		signal.Notify(ch, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
379		go func() {
380			<-ch
381			release()
382			os.Exit(130)
383		}()
384	}
385	
386	// ensureSelfSignedCert returns paths to a self-signed cert/key in dir,
387	// generating them if they don't already exist.
388	func ensureSelfSignedCert(dir string) (certFile, keyFile string, err error) {
389		if err = os.MkdirAll(dir, 0700); err != nil {
390			return
391		}
392		certFile = filepath.Join(dir, "cert.pem")
393		keyFile = filepath.Join(dir, "key.pem")
394	
395		// Re-use existing pair if both files are present and cert is still valid.

397			if _, e2 := os.Stat(keyFile); e2 == nil {
398				if pair, e3 := tls.LoadX509KeyPair(certFile, keyFile); e3 == nil {
399					leaf, e4 := x509.ParseCertificate(pair.Certificate[0])
400					if e4 == nil && time.Now().Before(leaf.NotAfter.Add(-24*time.Hour)) {

79605b15 fix(server): singleton guard + pid-alive validation for server.addr [ddx-61357e13]

commit 79605b155aa1ea5be9a6616b7bdb4f3c98b8c666
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sat May 2 15:22:05 2026 -0400

    fix(server): singleton guard + pid-alive validation for server.addr [ddx-61357e13]
    
    - Acquire mkdir-style lock at <XDG>/ddx/server.lock before listening; reject
      second launch with clear error naming holder pid + recorded URL.
    - Break the lock automatically when the recorded pid is dead.
    - Release the lock on SIGINT/SIGTERM so systemd restarts don't have to wait
      for stale-lock detection.
    - ReadServerAddr now validates the recorded pid is alive; on dead pid it
      warns to stderr, removes the stale file, and returns "" so callers fall
      back to the default 127.0.0.1:7743.

{"acceptance":"1. Server acquires flock on ~/.local/share/ddx/server.lock at startup. 2. Second 'ddx server' launch refuses with clear error message naming the existing pid + addr; exits non-zero. 3. Lock release on graceful shutdown (SIGTERM); stale-lock detection via dead-pid check on next startup. 4. writeAddrFile only runs after lock acquisition (file is consistent with lock holder). 5. ReadServerAddr (or its callers) validates recorded pid is alive before connecting. 6. If pid is dead, CLI emits a warning and falls back to default 127.0.0.1:7743. 7. New tests in cli/internal/server/: TestServer_SingletonGuardRefusesSecondLaunch + TestServer_StaleAddressFallbackWithWarning. 8. Manual verification: kill the live server; launch a new one; address-file matches the new server. Launch a second 'ddx server' while first is running; second refuses. 'ddx server workers list' succeeds against the live server after this fix.","claimed-at":"2026-05-02T19:32:35Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3446284","created_at":"2026-05-02T19:04:17.041000927Z","description":"Two related bugs causing 'ddx server workers list' (and other server-talking CLI commands) to fail with 'connection refused dial tcp 127.0.0.1:<stale-port>' even when a live server is running.

## Diagnosis

**writeAddrFile already exists** at cli/internal/server/server.go:287-312 and is called from line 273 on every startup. So the file IS written. The real bugs are:

**Bug A: No singleton-server guard.** Multiple 'ddx server' instances can start on the same machine concurrently. Each one calls writeAddrFile and overwrites the previous record. When one dies (e.g., a manual launch test), it leaves the address-file pointing to itself even though it's no longer running. systemd unit (Type=simple) prevents systemd-spawned duplicates of the same unit name but does NOT stop a manual 'ddx server' from racing.

Observed today: live systemd server PID 3442304 listening on 7743 (started ~14:27), but address file shows PID 3482077 / port 4174 (started ~15:01, died). Some manual or test launch overwrote the file then died.

**Bug B: CLI doesn't validate pid-alive before trusting the address file.** ReadServerAddr at cli/internal/server/server.go:328+ returns whatever's in the file. CLI commands then dial the recorded URL. If the recorded pid is dead, dial fails. No fallback to the systemd default (127.0.0.1:7743), no warning.

## Fix

Two halves, same surface (server bootstrap + CLI client read path):

**Server-side (singleton guard):**
- On startup, server acquires flock on ~/.local/share/ddx/server.lock
- If lock is held by an alive process: refuse to start with clear error ('ddx server already running, pid=<N>, addr=<URL>; only one ddx server per machine'). Exit non-zero.
- If lock is acquired (or stale — held by dead pid): proceed; release-on-exit handler clears the lock.
- writeAddrFile happens AFTER the lock acquires — so the address file always corresponds to the lock-holder.

**Client-side (defense in depth):**
- ReadServerAddr (or its callers) validates the recorded pid is alive (kill -0 equivalent on Unix, OpenProcess on Windows)
- If pid is dead: emit warning ('server.addr points to a dead pid <N>; falling back to default 127.0.0.1:7743') and use the default
- Optionally clear the stale file (best-effort; don't fail if unwritable)

## Out of scope

- Multi-server-per-machine support (the singleton guard is intentional — one server per machine is the design per FEAT-020)
- Federation: hub/spoke topology (Story 14) is multi-node, NOT multi-server-per-node","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-02T19:23:31.364112708Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260502T191634-fc858450","harness":"claude","input_tokens":44,"output_tokens":19686,"total_tokens":19730,"cost_usd":2.145058250000001,"duration_ms":415194,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-02T19:23:31.379899069Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=19730 cost_usd=2.1451"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-02T19:23:36.259204797Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"Diff contains only an execution result.json artifact; no code changes implementing the singleton flock guard, writeAddrFile ordering, pid-alive validation, or the required tests.
